ops.title: Fleet sweep: bare-sed plugin.json version extraction breaks when a dependencies block is added
ops.description: cc-context and cc-present both hit this on 2026-07-13: install-binary.sh (cc-guides pinned template) extracted the plugin version with `sed -n 's/.*"version": *"…"/\1/p'`, which matches every "version" line — a plugin.json `dependencies` entry (each carries its own "version" key) corrupts the extracted tag, 404ing SessionStart binary provisioning and failing release version-verify steps. The upstream template (cc-skills plugin/guides/sh/install-binary-pinned.sh) is fixed with `| head -n 1`; cc-context fixed its rendered copy + release.yml; cc-present fix in flight. SWEEP: every repo with a rendered install-binary.sh (pinned mode) or a release.yml verify step using the bare sed — re-render from the fixed template (or patch + lock) BEFORE that repo's plugin ever gains a dependencies block. Also check any other consumers of the pattern (release workflows, formula bumps).

ops.text: no repo in the fleet extracts the plugin version with an un-headed sed over plugin.json (grep sweep clean)
repos with cc-guides-managed copies re-rendered with lock updates
